info.title: kiapi ACE-Step API
info.description:
Music generation (ACE-Step 1.5): compose, cover, repaint, extract.

Four operations on ACE-Step 1.5: `/generate` (text2music — a new song from a
style prompt + lyrics), `/cover` (re-style an existing track), `/repaint`
(regenerate a time range), and `/extract` (separate into stems). ACE-Step runs in
an isolated subprocess (it pins an older transformers than chat/embedding); the
first request after idle pays a load cost of tens of seconds, then it stays
resident until the idle TTL or budget pressure frees it.

## Upstream docs
- [ACE-Step](https://github.com/ace-step/ACE-Step) — the upstream model

## Models
- **xl-base** (default) — highest quality. 32 inference steps, CFG 7.0.
  ~25 s per 30 s of audio on M4 Max.
- **turbo** — fastest. 8 inference steps, no CFG. ~4 s per 15 s on M4 Max. Great
  for prototyping / iterating on prompts.

Pick the preset with `model`; discover variants at `GET /v1/audio/acestep/models`.
The sampling overrides (`inference_steps`, `guidance_scale`, `shift`) default to
the preset's tuned values — set them only to deviate.

## Source workflow
`/cover`, `/repaint`, and `/extract` operate on an existing track: POST the source
WAV to `/v1/files` to get a `file_id`, then pass it as `source`. `/generate` needs
no source.

## Prompt tips
Describe the SOUND, not the story — a narrative sentence beats a keyword list.
Useful elements to mention:
- **genre**: Modern J-Pop / Acoustic Jazz / Dark Trap / Lo-fi Hip-hop / Anime OST
- **tempo**: 132 BPM / slow and intimate / driving beat / half-time feel
- **instruments**: bright piano / smooth saxophone / 808 sub-bass / nylon guitar
- **vocal**: emotional female vocal / whispered delivery / powerful male tenor
- **mood**: melancholic / triumphant / cozy / aggressive / dreamy / nostalgic
- **production**: polished radio-ready / lo-fi vinyl texture / orchestral / live band

- **good**: "A melancholic piano ballad where soft female vocals weave through
  gentle strings, intimate and heartbreaking. 80 BPM."
- **bad**: "piano, sad, female, slow"

## Lyrics format (generate)
Use `[Square Bracket]` section tags at the start of a line: `[Intro]`,
`[Verse 1]`, `[Pre-Chorus]`, `[Chorus]`, `[Bridge]`, `[Instrumental]`, `[Outro]`.
Modifiers work too, e.g. `[Verse 1 - Female]`, `[Chorus - Both]`,
`[Bridge - Whispered]`. Set lyrics to `[Instrumental]` for no vocals. Keep the
lyric length proportionate to `duration` — sparse lyrics over a long duration
degrade quality.

## Language codes (lang, generate)
ISO 639-1: `ja` Japanese, `en` English, `ko` Korean, `zh` Mandarin, `yue`
Cantonese, `es` Spanish, `fr` French, `de` German, …. `unknown` auto-detects
(may reduce quality).

## TIPS
- For a quick track, call `sync` without `Accept: application/json` to get the raw
  WAV bytes straight back (`curl -o out.wav`). `/extract` is multi-artifact, so it
  always returns Job JSON.
- Reuse a `seed` (≠ -1) with the same params to reproduce a result.
- Run `kiapi activate` ahead of time so the first request doesn't pay a cold-start
  weight download.

## Examples

### Generate (sync, turbo)
```sh
jq -n \
--arg prompt "Modern J-Pop, 132 BPM, bright piano, emotional electric guitar, upbeat drums, polished anime opening production" \
--arg lyrics '[Verse 1]
加速する世界の中で
君の声が聴こえてくる

[Pre-Chorus]
夜明け前の空に
まだ見ぬ明日を描いた

[Chorus]
僕らは光を追いかける
終わらない夢の向こうへ
何度でも手を伸ばして
新しい風になる
' \
'{
  "model": "turbo",
  "mode": "sync",
  "prompt": $prompt,
  "lyrics": $lyrics,
  "duration": 30,
  "lang": "ja",
  "seed": 1
}' |
curl -sS -X POST http://localhost:${PORT:-8000}/v1/audio/acestep/generate \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data-binary @- \
  -o acestep_generate.wav
```

### Cover (async, turbo)
```sh
curl -sS http://HOST:PORT/v1/audio/acestep/cover \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "model": "turbo",
    "mode": "async",
    "source": "<file_id>",
    "prompt": "lo-fi chillhop, warm tape",
    "strength": 0.7
  }'
# -> {"job_id": "..."}; poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}
```

### Extract stems
```sh
curl -sS http://HOST:PORT/v1/audio/acestep/extract \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "source": "<file_id>",
    "targets": ["vocals", "drums", "bass", "other"]
  }'
# -> one job, artifacts = 4 file_ids (one WAV per target)
```

post.description:
Generate a brand-new song from a style prompt + lyrics (text2music).

Takes no source track: `prompt` describes the SOUND, `lyrics` carries the
section-tagged words (or `[Instrumental]`), and `duration`/`lang` shape the
output. To restyle or edit an existing track use `/cover`, `/repaint`, or
`/extract` instead. The same endpoint serves both `sync` and `async` via
`mode`.

Sync content negotiation: one WAV is produced, so unless the client asks for
JSON the raw audio bytes are returned with `X-Kiapi-File-Id` / `X-Kiapi-Job-Id`
headers — `curl -o out.wav` just works. With `Accept: application/json` (or
async) the Job JSON is returned, whose `result` follows TrackResponse.

Async returns 202 immediately; poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} and fetch the
artifact via GET /v1/files/{file_id}.

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

post.description:
Re-style an existing track while preserving its structure (cover).

Requires a `source` track and a `prompt` describing the target style;
`strength` (0..1) trades creative freedom against faithfulness to the source.
To regenerate only a time range use `/repaint`; to make a song from scratch
use `/generate`. The same endpoint serves both `sync` and `async` via `mode`.

Sync content negotiation and the TrackResponse `result` shape match
`/v1/audio/acestep/generate` (one WAV artifact; `result.src` references the
source file_id).

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

post.description:
Regenerate one time range of an existing track (repaint).

Requires a `source` track, a `prompt` for the section's style, and a
`start`/`end` window (`end=-1` runs to the end of the track); `strength`
(0..1) controls how aggressively that window is regenerated while it blends
into the rest. To restyle the whole track use `/cover`. The same endpoint
serves both `sync` and `async` via `mode`.

Sync content negotiation and the TrackResponse `result` shape match
`/v1/audio/acestep/generate` (one WAV artifact; `result.src` references the
source file_id).

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

post.description:
Separate a track into stems / source separation (extract).

Requires a `source` track and a list of `targets` (e.g. vocals / drums / bass
/ other). One job produces one WAV stem per target, so `artifacts` holds a
file_id per target. The same endpoint serves both `sync` and `async` via
`mode`.

Unlike the other operations this is a multi-artifact job, so sync always
returns the Job JSON (no raw-bytes shortcut). The `result` follows
ExtractResponse, whose `stems[]` carry one file_id each. Fetch each via
GET /v1/files/{file_id}.

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

get.description:
List the servable models for this capability.

Returns the public catalog of every variant selectable via the ``model``
field on this capability's endpoints.

schema.title: Response List Models V1 Audio Acestep Models Get

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: In-memory job id. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} to inspect status, progress, result, and artifacts.

type.title: Type
type.description: Job type. Generation APIs use values such as zimage, flux2-edit, or acestep-extract.

status.description: Initial job status. Async responses are normally queued unless the worker starts immediately.

name.title: Name
name.description: Model variant name to pass in the request model field.

family.title: Family
family.description: Capability family that resolves this model variant.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Capability domain used for grouping model lists.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Alternative names that also resolve to this model.

default.title: Default
default.description: Whether this is the default model when the request omits model.

features.title: Features
features.description: Handler-declared modalities and features supported by this model.

CapabilityModelSpec.title: CapabilityModelSpec
CapabilityModelSpec.description: Public model discovery entry for capability-specific model lists.

model.title: Model
model.description: Preset (see GET /v1/audio/acestep/models). `xl-base` (default) = highest quality (32 steps, CFG 7.0). `turbo` = fastest (8 steps, no CFG), good for prototyping.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the track (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. `-1` picks a random seed; the same seed with the same params reproduces the output. The resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`.

inference_steps.title: Inference Steps
inference_steps.description: Override the preset's denoising step count (xl-base 32 / turbo 8). More steps = higher quality, slower. Omit to use the preset default.

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: Override classifier-free guidance strength (xl-base default 7.0). Higher follows the prompt more strictly. Ignored by `turbo`, which runs without CFG. Omit to use the preset default.

shift.title: Shift
shift.description: Override the timestep schedule shift. 3.0 is correct; values near 1.0 produce noisy output. Omit to use the preset default.

source.description: Source audio to re-style (Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL). POST the WAV to /v1/files first to get a file id.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Target style description for the cover — the SOUND to move toward (genre, instruments, mood, production).

strength.title: Strength
strength.description: How closely to follow the source structure, in 0..1. 0 = free / more creative, 1 = strict / more faithful. 0.7 preserves structure well.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Output length in seconds (5-300). Omit to match the source length.

model.title: Model
model.description: Preset (see GET /v1/audio/acestep/models). `xl-base` (default) = highest quality (32 steps, CFG 7.0). `turbo` = fastest (8 steps, no CFG), good for prototyping.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the track (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. `-1` picks a random seed; the same seed with the same params reproduces the output. The resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`.

inference_steps.title: Inference Steps
inference_steps.description: Override the preset's denoising step count (xl-base 32 / turbo 8). More steps = higher quality, slower. Omit to use the preset default.

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: Override classifier-free guidance strength (xl-base default 7.0). Higher follows the prompt more strictly. Ignored by `turbo`, which runs without CFG. Omit to use the preset default.

shift.title: Shift
shift.description: Override the timestep schedule shift. 3.0 is correct; values near 1.0 produce noisy output. Omit to use the preset default.

source.description: Source audio to separate (Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL). POST the WAV to /v1/files first to get a file id.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: Stems to separate out, e.g. vocals / drums / bass / other (default: all four). Each target becomes one artifact (file_id) within the single job.

source_file_id.title: Source File Id
source_file_id.description: Files-API id of the source track that was separated.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: The requested stems, in the order they appear in `stems`.

stems.title: Stems
stems.description: One produced stem per target; each references its own WAV file_id.

ExtractResponse.title: ExtractResponse
ExtractResponse.description:
Capability-specific ``result`` for a succeeded extract job.

A single job produces one stem per requested target; ``artifacts`` lists the
same file_ids in ``stems`` order.

model.title: Model
model.description: Preset (see GET /v1/audio/acestep/models). `xl-base` (default) = highest quality (32 steps, CFG 7.0). `turbo` = fastest (8 steps, no CFG), good for prototyping.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the track (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. `-1` picks a random seed; the same seed with the same params reproduces the output. The resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`.

inference_steps.title: Inference Steps
inference_steps.description: Override the preset's denoising step count (xl-base 32 / turbo 8). More steps = higher quality, slower. Omit to use the preset default.

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: Override classifier-free guidance strength (xl-base default 7.0). Higher follows the prompt more strictly. Ignored by `turbo`, which runs without CFG. Omit to use the preset default.

shift.title: Shift
shift.description: Override the timestep schedule shift. 3.0 is correct; values near 1.0 produce noisy output. Omit to use the preset default.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Music style description — the SOUND (genre, tempo, instruments, mood, production), not the lyric content. A narrative sentence works better than a keyword list.
prompt.default: Modern J-Pop, 132 BPM, bright piano, emotional electric guitar, upbeat drums

lyrics.title: Lyrics
lyrics.description: Lyrics, using [Verse 1]/[Chorus]/[Bridge]/… section tags at the start of a line. Use `[Instrumental]` for a fully instrumental output. Match the lyric length to `duration` — sparse lyrics over a long duration hurt quality.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Output length in seconds (5-300). Also capped server-side by the acestep `max_duration` setting (422 if exceeded).

lang.title: Lang
lang.description: Vocal language as an ISO 639-1 code: ja / en / ko / zh / yue / es / fr / de / …. `unknown` auto-detects (may reduce quality). Ignored for `[Instrumental]` lyrics.

type.description: Job type. Use this to interpret the capability-specific result payload.

params.title: Params
params.description: Request parameters captured for inspection and reproducibility. Secret or large media payloads may be omitted or redacted by endpoints.

id.description: In-memory job id. Jobs are cleared when the kiapi process restarts.

status.description: Job lifecycle state: queued, running, succeeded, failed, or canceled.

artifacts.title: Artifacts
artifacts.description: File ids produced by the job. Use GET /v1/files/{file_id} for metadata or /download for bytes.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message when status is failed; otherwise null.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job was created.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: Unix timestamp when the worker started the job, or null while queued.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job reached a terminal state, or null while queued/running.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Best-effort completion fraction in [0.0, 1.0]. Null means the job has not reported progress.

progress_label.title: Progress Label
progress_label.description: Short human-readable phase label such as queued, running, denoising, saving, or done.

type.description: Job type. Use this to interpret the capability-specific result payload.

params.title: Params
params.description: Request parameters captured for inspection and reproducibility. Secret or large media payloads may be omitted or redacted by endpoints.

id.description: In-memory job id. Jobs are cleared when the kiapi process restarts.

status.description: Job lifecycle state: queued, running, succeeded, failed, or canceled.

artifacts.title: Artifacts
artifacts.description: File ids produced by the job. Use GET /v1/files/{file_id} for metadata or /download for bytes.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message when status is failed; otherwise null.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job was created.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: Unix timestamp when the worker started the job, or null while queued.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job reached a terminal state, or null while queued/running.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Best-effort completion fraction in [0.0, 1.0]. Null means the job has not reported progress.

progress_label.title: Progress Label
progress_label.description: Short human-readable phase label such as queued, running, denoising, saving, or done.

model.title: Model
model.description: Preset (see GET /v1/audio/acestep/models). `xl-base` (default) = highest quality (32 steps, CFG 7.0). `turbo` = fastest (8 steps, no CFG), good for prototyping.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the track (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. `-1` picks a random seed; the same seed with the same params reproduces the output. The resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`.

inference_steps.title: Inference Steps
inference_steps.description: Override the preset's denoising step count (xl-base 32 / turbo 8). More steps = higher quality, slower. Omit to use the preset default.

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: Override classifier-free guidance strength (xl-base default 7.0). Higher follows the prompt more strictly. Ignored by `turbo`, which runs without CFG. Omit to use the preset default.

shift.title: Shift
shift.description: Override the timestep schedule shift. 3.0 is correct; values near 1.0 produce noisy output. Omit to use the preset default.

source.description: Source audio to repaint (Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL). POST the WAV to /v1/files first to get a file id.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Style description for the repainted section (the SOUND to apply).

start.title: Start
start.description: Start of the section to regenerate, in seconds from the track start.

end.title: End
end.description: End of the section, in seconds. `-1` repaints from `start` to the end of the track.

strength.title: Strength
strength.description: How aggressively to regenerate the section, in 0..1. 0 = subtle, blends into the rest; 1 = aggressive.

task.title: Task
task.description: Operation that produced the track: text2music, cover, or repaint.

model.title: Model
model.description: Resolved preset that produced the track (xl-base or turbo).

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Files-API id of the produced WAV. Fetch metadata at GET /v1/files/{id} or bytes at /download. This is also the artifact returned as raw bytes by a single-artifact sync call.

audio_bytes.title: Audio Bytes
audio_bytes.description: Size of the produced WAV in bytes.

src.title: Src
src.description: Files-API id of the source track. Present for cover/repaint; null for generate (which takes no source).

params.title: Params
params.description: Resolved parameters actually used for the run (prompt, lyrics, duration, seed, inference_steps, guidance_scale, shift, …), so the result is reproducible.

TrackResponse.title: TrackResponse
TrackResponse.description: Capability-specific ``result`` for a succeeded generate/cover/repaint job.

target.title: Target
target.description: Stem name, e.g. vocals / drums / bass / other.

model.title: Model
model.description: Resolved preset that produced the stem.

src.title: Src
src.description: Files-API id of the source track.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Files-API id of this stem's WAV. Fetch metadata at GET /v1/files/{id} or bytes at /download.

audio_bytes.title: Audio Bytes
audio_bytes.description: Size of the stem WAV in bytes.

params.title: Params
params.description: Resolved parameters used to separate this stem.

total_s.title: Total S
total_s.description: Wall-clock separation time in seconds for this stem.

total_s.title: Total S
total_s.description: Wall-clock generation time in seconds (subprocess run only).
